title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot expresses his belief that the investigation is not yet over, noting that unexplained problems remain. He asks Mrs. Hubbard to call Patricia Lane so he can examine a stolen ring. Patricia, an archaeologist in her early thirties, hands Poirot her mother's out-of-fashion diamond engagement ring. During their conversation, Patricia expresses regret regarding her relationship with her deceased parents and explains her serious nature. She defends Nigel against accusations regarding the incident involving Miss Johnston, suggesting someone used green ink to frame him. Patricia reveals she met Nigel a year ago at the Chateaux de la Loire, where she cared for him while he suffered from pneumonia. Poirot, finding Patricia's personality somewhat monotonous, takes the ring for further examination, promising to return it the next day and warning her to be careful.

The following morning, Mrs. Hubbard finds the breakfast atmosphere tense due to the discovery that Elizabeth Johnston's paper was ruined. Mr. Chandra Lal interprets the incident as an act of racial oppression, leading to a heated argument involving Gene Tomlinson, Valerie Hobhouse, and Nigel. Nigel mockingly claims responsibility for spilling the ink to protect Patricia, but Patricia clarifies that she was the one who borrowed ink from him the previous day. The argument escalates into a political debate involving the Oxford Group and the Mau Mau Uprising, prompting Nigel to leave the room in frustration. Patricia follows him outside, drawing a sneer from Valerie. Meanwhile, Genevieve, a French girl, reacts with a sudden scream after receiving a translation from Rene.
